question_text: Gravitational field strength on Earth is approximately 10 N/kg. What does this tell us?

options.label: A
options.text: Every kilogram of mass experiences a gravitational force of 10 newtons

options.label: C
options.text: The Earth's gravity is 10 times stronger than the Moon's gravity

options.label: D
options.text: All objects fall at 10 metres per second

explanation: Gravitational field strength tells us how much gravitational force acts on each kilogram of mass. At 10 N/kg, every 1 kg of mass experiences a downward force of 10 newtons due to Earth's gravity.
